body:
## Summary

Implements Google Docs document capture support, addressing all requirements from issue #36:

- **Google Docs capture module** (`gdocs.js` / `gdocs.rs`): Detects Google Docs URLs, extracts document IDs, and fetches documents via the Google Docs export URL pattern (`/export?format=html|md|txt|pdf|docx|epub|zip`)
- **API token support**: `--apiToken` CLI option and `API_TOKEN` environment variable for private document access. API server supports `Authorization: Bearer <token>` and `X-Api-Token` headers
- **`/gdocs` API endpoint**: New endpoint in both Express.js and Axum servers
- **Auto-detection**: CLI automatically detects Google Docs URLs and uses API-based capture
- **Configurable capture**: `--capture` option to choose between `browser` and `api` capture methods
- **Archive mode**: `format=archive` extracts embedded base64 images from Google Docs HTML exports and bundles them with markdown and HTML into a ZIP archive (`article.md`, `article.html`, `images/`)
- **HTML entity decoding**: All HTML and Markdown extraction pipelines decode HTML entities to unicode characters (e.g., `&` → `&`, `—` → `—`) using `html-escape` (Rust) and `he` (JS)
- **Unicode by default**: All export methods request data in UTF-8 and convert to unicode
- **Rust tests reorganized**: All tests moved from inline `#[cfg(test)]` modules in `src/` to separate test files in `tests/` folder for better visibility and organization
- **lino-arguments updated** from `^0.2.1` to `^0.2.5` (latest)
- **JS and Rust in sync**: Both implementations have identical functionality

## Changes

### Google Docs Module
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `js/src/gdocs.js` | Google Docs capture: URL detection, export URL construction, fetch, archive mode, image extraction |
| `rust/src/gdocs.rs` | Rust Google Docs capture module (matching JS) with archive and image extraction |
| `js/src/index.js` | Register `/gdocs` endpoint |
| `rust/src/main.rs` | Add `/gdocs` endpoint with archive/zip format support |

### HTML Entity Decoding
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `js/src/lib.js` | Add `he.decode()` to HTML-to-Markdown conversion pipelines |
| `js/src/gdocs.js` | Decode HTML entities for text-based export formats |
| `rust/src/html.rs` | Add `decode_html_entities()` and `normalize_url()` public functions |
| `rust/src/markdown.rs` | Decode HTML entities after html2md conversion |
| `rust/src/gdocs.rs` | Decode entities for text-based formats, unicode request headers |
| `rust/Cargo.toml` | Add `html-escape` and `zip` dependencies |
| `js/package.json` | Add `he` dependency |

### Test Reorganization
| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `rust/tests/*_tests.rs` | 14 test files moved from inline modules (110+ tests total) |
| `js/tests/unit/gdocs.test.js` | 27 unit tests including image extraction tests |

## Test plan

- [x] Rust: 110 tests pass across 14 test files (0 failures)
- [x] Rust: clippy clean (pedantic + nursery lints)
- [x] JS: 188 unit tests pass (0 failures)
- [x] JS: 16 browser tests skipped (pre-existing, require Chromium)
- [x] HTML entity decoding tested for basic entities, unicode, and named entities
- [x] Base64 image extraction tested for single/multiple/no images and mixed content
- [ ] Manual test: `web-capture https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit --format markdown`
- [ ] Manual test: API `/gdocs?url=...&format=archive` returns ZIP with images

### Usage examples

```bash
# CLI: Capture public Google Doc as Markdown
web-capture https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit --format markdown

# CLI: Capture as archive (ZIP with markdown + HTML + images)
web-capture https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit --format archive

# API: Get Markdown
GET /gdocs?url=https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit&format=markdown

# API: Get archive with images
GET /gdocs?url=https://docs.google.com/document/d/DOC_ID/edit&format=archive

# API: With auth for private documents
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" "http://localhost:3000/gdocs?url=...&format=archive"
```

Fixes #36

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add Google Docs (document to markdown) support

## Summary
- remove the JavaScript and Rust HTTP /gdocs route registrations and server endpoint listings
- delete the now-unused /gdocs handler code while preserving Google Docs source capture helpers for normal output formats
- remove /gdocs from endpoint documentation and add issue #73 case-study artifacts
- add a patch changeset for the public API route removal

## Tests
- cargo fmt -- --check
- cargo test
- npx prettier --check src/index.js src/gdocs.js bin/web-capture.js tests/integration/api-endpoints.test.js tests/unit/cli.test.js ../README.md ../rust/README.md ../docs/case-studies/issue-73/README.md
- node scripts/validate-changeset.mjs
- JS Jest target could not be run locally because this checkout has no js/node_modules and the available node binary is v20.20.2 while package.json requires Node >=22 <23

Fixes #73
title: fix: remove /gdocs API route

body:
## Summary

Fixes #53 — six bugs in the Google Docs capture path that made `--archive zip` and default markdown mode unusable:

- **Bug 1 & 5**: `--archive zip` wrote raw HTML bytes instead of a real zip archive (both with `-o` and auto-derived paths)
- **Bug 2**: Default markdown mode silently stripped all base64 images from Google Docs (which only has data URIs), producing lossy output with zero images
- **Bug 3**: `WEB_CAPTURE_KEEP_ORIGINAL_LINKS=false` was ignored — images were always stripped on the gdocs path
- **Bug 4**: Auto-derived archive path used `document.archive` instead of `document.zip`

## Root Causes

### Rust (`rust/src/main.rs`)
- `capture_url()` passed `"archive"` as the format to the Google Docs match, but the match only handled `"markdown"` — falling through to the `_` catch-all which wrote raw HTML directly
- The markdown path called `strip_base64_images()` when `!embed_images` (default), discarding all images. It never checked `keep_original_links` and never called `extract_and_save_images()`

### JavaScript (`js/bin/web-capture.js`, `js/src/markdown.js`)
- Same structural bugs: no `"archive"` handling for Google Docs URLs, and markdown mode stripped instead of extracting

## Changes

### Rust
- Added `"archive"` match arm for Google Docs → calls `fetch_google_doc_as_archive()` + `create_archive_zip()`
- Added `"archive"` match arm for regular URLs → uses `extract_base64_to_buffers()` + `create_archive_zip()`
- Fixed markdown mode: extracts base64 images to `images/` dir by default, strips only when `--keep-original-links` is explicitly set
- Uses correct archive extension (`zip`/`7z`/`tar.gz`/`tar`) in auto-derived paths
- Fixed API `/markdown` handler to respect `embedImages` parameter correctly

### JavaScript
- Added `archive`/`zip` format handling for Google Docs URLs using `fetchGoogleDocAsArchive()`
- Fixed markdown mode: extracts base64 images to `images/` dir by default (both gdocs and regular paths)
- Fixed `keepOriginalLinks` logic: only strip when explicitly requested
- Fixed API markdown handler: removed unused `keepOriginalLinks` variable, simplified strip logic

### Tests
- **Rust**: 2 new tests verifying `create_archive_zip` produces valid ZIP with correct contents (article.md, article.html, images/)
- **JS**: 4 new tests verifying the image extraction pipeline (HTML extraction, disk extraction, stripping, buffer extraction)
- All 85 Rust tests pass, 194/200 JS tests pass (6 pre-existing failures from missing Chrome/Playwright binaries)

## Test Plan

- [x] `cargo check` — compiles clean
- [x] `cargo test --test unit --test integration` — 85/85 pass
- [x] `cargo clippy` — only pre-existing warning (unused `keep_original_links` field in `MarkdownQuery`)
- [x] JS unit tests — 194/200 pass (6 browser.test.js failures are pre-existing env issue)
- [x] ESLint — 0 errors (6 pre-existing warnings for function complexity)
- [x] Prettier — formatting verified
- [ ] Manual test with real Google Docs URL: `web-capture --archive zip <gdocs-url>` → should produce a real zip
- [ ] Manual test: `web-capture <gdocs-url>` → should create `document.md` + `images/` directory

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: fix: Google Docs archive produces real zip, default markdown extracts images

body:
## Summary

Fixes all five archive quality bugs reported in #58 for `web-capture v0.2.1`:

- **Bug A**: Markdown image refs now use relative `images/image-NN.ext` paths instead of fabricated absolute URLs. Root cause: the archive pipeline was passing the Google Docs export URL as base URL to `convert_html_to_markdown`, which resolved already-localized `images/` paths back to non-existent absolute URLs.
- **Bug B**: Image count mismatch between MD and HTML resolved as a consequence of the Bug A fix — the URL resolution was breaking some image references.
- **Bug C**: HTML output is now pretty-printed with indentation by default. Opt-out via `--no-pretty-html` flag (both Rust and JS CLI) or `WEB_CAPTURE_NO_PRETTY_HTML=true` env var.
- **Bug D**: Non-breaking spaces (` `) are preserved as ` ` entities in Markdown output for clear marking — HTML decoding does not make breaking changes to the markup.
- **Bug E**: All internal filenames renamed from `article.*` to `document.*` — archive contents (`document.md`, `document.html`), batch config defaults, and documentation.
- **JS parity**: All fixes and flags applied identically in both Rust and JavaScript implementations.
- **Test file naming**: Rust test files renamed to remove `_tests` suffix (follow JS convention — files are already in `tests/` directory).

## Files changed

### Rust
- `rust/src/gdocs.rs` — Pass `None` as base URL for archive markdown; rename `article.*` → `document.*`; add `pretty_html` parameter to `create_archive_zip`
- `rust/src/html.rs` — Add `pretty_print_html()` function
- `rust/src/markdown.rs` — Preserve ` ` entities instead of normalizing to regular spaces
- `rust/src/postprocess.rs` — Preserve ` ` entities in unicode normalization
- `rust/src/batch.rs` — Rename default filenames to `document.*`
- `rust/src/main.rs` — Add `--no-pretty-html` flag; pass `pretty_html` to archive creation
- `rust/tests/` — Rename all `*_tests.rs` → `*.rs` (remove suffix)

### JavaScript
- `js/src/gdocs.js` — Pass no base URL for archive markdown; rename `article.*` → `document.*`; apply pretty-printing
- `js/src/lib.js` — Add `prettyPrintHtml()` function; preserve ` ` entities
- `js/src/postprocess.js` — Preserve ` ` entities in unicode normalization
- `js/src/archive.js` — Rename filenames; apply pretty-printing
- `js/src/batch.js` — Rename default filenames
- `js/bin/web-capture.js` — Add `--no-pretty-html` flag; rename filenames; conditionally apply pretty-printing

### Tests & Docs
- Updated all test assertions for `document.*` filenames and ` ` preservation
- Added pretty-print HTML tests
- Case study documentation in `docs/case-studies/issue-58/`

## Test plan

- [x] All 88 Rust unit tests pass
- [x] All 40 Rust integration tests pass
- [x] All 196 JS unit tests pass (4 browser tests skipped — pre-existing, requires browser)
- [ ] Verify with real Google Docs URLs that archive contents have correct relative image paths
- [ ] Verify HTML output is indented/pretty-printed
- [ ] Verify no `article.*` filenames remain in outputs

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)


Fixes #58
title: fix: resolve archive quality bugs A-E (issue #58)

body:
## Summary

Addresses feedback from [review comment](https://github.com/link-assistant/web-capture/pull/49#issuecomment-4242509381):

- **Markdown mode** (API `/markdown` + CLI `-f markdown`): `keepOriginalLinks=true`, `embedImages=false` by default — outputs clean single-file markdown with original remote image URLs preserved and base64 data URIs stripped
- **Archive mode** (API `/archive` + CLI `--archive`): `keepOriginalLinks=false`, `embedImages=false`, `localImages=true` by default — downloads images to `images/` folder
- **API/CLI parity**: Identical default behavior across API and CLI for all modes
- **Removed image extraction to files from markdown mode**: Markdown mode produces a single file only, no `images/` folder
- **Removed redundant test files**: `rust/tests/unit.rs` and `rust/tests/integration.rs` replaced by `[[test]]` sections in `Cargo.toml`

## Default Behavior

| Mode | `keepOriginalLinks` | `embedImages` | `localImages` | Result |
|------|---------------------|---------------|---------------|--------|
| Markdown (API + CLI) | `true` | `false` | N/A | Clean markdown, original remote URLs, no base64 |
| Archive (API + CLI) | `false` | `false` | `true` | ZIP with `article.md` + `images/` folder |

## Changes

### JavaScript
- `js/src/markdown.js`: `keepOriginalLinks` defaults to `true`, `embedImages` defaults to `false`
- `js/bin/web-capture.js`: Markdown mode always strips base64 (no image extraction to files)

### Rust
- `rust/src/main.rs`: `MarkdownQuery` defaults `keep_original_links=true`, `embed_images=false`; CLI markdown path simplified
- `rust/Cargo.toml`: Added `[[test]]` sections for unit and integration tests
- `rust/tests/unit.rs`, `rust/tests/integration.rs`: Removed (replaced by Cargo.toml entries)

### Documentation
- `README.md`, `js/README.md`, `rust/README.md`: Updated API endpoint descriptions and defaults

## Test plan

- [x] Rust unit tests: 85 pass, 0 fail
- [x] Rust clippy: 0 warnings
- [x] Rust fmt: clean
- [x] JS unit tests: 192 pass (4 pre-existing Playwright browser failures unrelated)
- [x] ESLint: 0 errors
- [x] Prettier: clean
- [ ] CI: running

Fixes #48

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Extract images to files in archive mode, keep original links in markdown mode
